titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/39

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=515 status=live modified=2021-04-06 11:28:22.649595+00 | translation moa=328 status=live modified=2024-03-05 14:35:59.762473+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis . tam presentibus quam futuris . Rob’ Auenel salutem .

Sciant omnes quod ego Rob’ Auenel . annuente domino meo Willelmo Rege Scott’ . per concessum et assensum Geruasii heredis mei
concessi et dedi . et hac mea carta confirmaui deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Meylros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . terram meam de Erchedale . scilicet Tumloher . et Weidkerroc . in puram et liberam
et perpetuam elemosinam . pro anima domini mei Regis Dauid . qui eandem terram dedit mihi pro seruicio meo . et pro anima comitis Henrici . et Malcolmi Regis . et pro anima domini mei Willelmi Regis Scott’ . et omnium
antecessorum et successorum suorum . et pro anima mea . et Sibbille uxoris mee . et pro animabus omnium heredum et parentum meorum . Hanc terram . scilicet Tumloher et Weidkerroc . dedi predictis
monachis . et incartaui . per has diuisas . videlicet sicut duo esches conueniunt . et ita sursum per blac esch . usque ad primum torrentem qui descendit de hertesheued . et de illo torrente sursum per
medium cundos montium . inter ipsos et Robertum de brus . sicut aque descendunt uersus ipsos monachos . Et ita inter forestam de Thimei et ipsos . per medium cundos montium . sicut aque descendunt
uersus ipsos . Similiter inter terram de bordewich et ipsos monachos . per medium cundos montium . sicut aque descendunt uersus ipsos . Et ita usque ad terram de hawic . et inde in transuersum per medium
cundos montium quod est inter meipsum et predictos monachos . sicut aque descendunt uersus ipsos . Quod cundos se extendit per desursum harewude . et sic descendit uesque ubi duo esches conueiunt . Totam autem hanc prenominatam terram . et possessionem . et quicquid continetur infra has prescriptas diuisas . in bosco et plano . in pratis et pasturis . in aquis et mariscis . et in omnibus utilitatibus . et
aisiamentis . concessi et dedi . et confirmaui antedictis monachis . in puram et perpetuam elemosinam . tenore perpetuo tenendam et possidendam de me et heredibus meis . Liberam et quietam
ab omnibus seruiciis . et ab omni exactione seculari . sicut liberius et quietius . plenius et honorificentius . aliqua elemosina teneri et possideri potest . Et si forte predictam elemosinam calumpniari aliquis
uoluerit . ego et heredes mei omni calumpnianti respondebimus . et sufficienter defendemus eam predictis monachis . et warantizabimus . Hoc autem sciendum quod mihi et heredibus meis retinui tantum
ceruum et ceruam . porcum et capriolum, infra has prescriptas diuisas . absque dampno tamen et lesione segetum . pratorum . sepium . pecudum . animalium . et omnium aliarum possessioneum . eorundem monachorum .
Ita tamen . quod ego et heredes mei forestarium non habebimus infra has prefatas diuisas . verumptamen habebimus infra easdem diuisas latrones . et accipitrum et sparueriorum nidos . et custodes ad illos
custodiendos . et forseueras . Sciendum etiam quod predicti monachi habebunt et possidebunt totam predictam terram liberam et quietam a me et heredibus meis . salua predicta saluagina mea . et
nidis accipitrum et sparueriorum . Et utentur omni hoc tenemento pro libitum et uoluntate sua . sicut melius et utilius . sibi et fratribus suis . et seruientibus et aueriis suis . proficere uisum fuerit . Excepto
quod non uenabuntur ibi cum motis et cordis . nec alios ducet ad uenandum . nec pedicas ibi ponent . nisi ad capiendos lupos . neque accipient infra has diuisas . accipitrum et sparueriorum nidos .
Preterea sciendum quod monachi prenominati solebant reddere mihi annuatim quinque marcas . quando dedi eis primo hanc terram . sed post aliquot annos . ad honorem dei et pro salute anime mee remisi
et dedi eis quintam marcam in elemosinam . ad procurandum lumen ante altare sancte Marie . imperpetuum . Deinde post obitum sibille uxoris mee . per assensum et concessum Geruasii heredis mei . et per consilium
amicorum meorum . ex toto remisi et dedi deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de meylros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus in puram et perpetuam elemosinam . et quietas clamaui ipsas quatuor marcas . quas
pluribus annis mihi reddere solebant . Hanc igitur pensionem . et totam prescriptam possessionem . assentiente et concedente Geruasio herede meo . dedi deo et predictis monachis . in liberam et puram . et
perpetuam elemosinam . quietam et solutam ab omni seruicio et exactione seculari . Et ut hec mea elemosina stabiles et firma sit imperpetuum . nec ullo modo possit in irritum duci . renouaui
et feci eisdem monachis hanc meam cartam . in tempore domini mei Willelmi Regis scottorum . et dominus meus Willelmus rex . petitione et rogatu meo . hanc meam donationem et elemosinam carta
sua confirmauit . sicut dominus meus Rex Malcolmus . rogatu meo illam priorem concessit . et carta sua confirmauit . Prioris ante donationis mee et carte quam feci eis tempore domini mei Malcolmi
regis .

hi sunt testes . Ric . episcopus sancti And’ . Engelr’ episcopus de Glasg’ . Greg’ . episcopus de Dunkel’ . Gaufridus abbas de dunferm’ . Iohannes abbas de Kelcho’ . Aluredus abbas de striuel’ . Nicholaus
cancellarius . Matheus archidiaconus . Walleuus comes . Dunec’ . comes . Ferted comes . Malcomus comes . Ric’ de morauill’ . Walterus filius Alani . Dauid olifard . Rob’ de quinci . Ric’ cumin .
Bernardus filius brie’ . Rob’ de berkel’ . Walterus clericus . Ric’ clericus . Walterus de berkel’ . Sed post ea tempore domini mei Willelmi Regis . quando hec donatio renouata est . et firma predicta
ex toto remissa est . isti etiam testes sunt . Iocelinus episcopus Glasg’ . Symon archidiac’ . hugo paruus clericus regis . Willelmus senescallus episcopi . Walterus clericus episcopi . Dauid comes . frater Regis . Scot’ . Alanus
senescallus Regis Scot’ . Robertus le croc miles ipsius . Alanus filius hugonis . senescallus comitis . Dauid . Herbertus de arches . Willelmus de aldri . Geruasius de eincurt . nepos domini Roberti . Glai nepos ipsius .
To all sons of holy Mother Church, as well present as future, Robert Avenel sends greeting.

Let all people know that I, Robert Avenel, with my lord William king of the Scots approving, by the consent and agreement of my heir, Gervase, have granted and given, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks in that place serving God, my land of Eskdale, that is, Tomleuchar, and Watcarrick, in pure and free and everlasting alms, for the soul of my lord, King David, who gave the same land to me in return for my service, and for the soul of Earl Henry, and of King Malcolm, and for the soul of my lord William, king of the Scots, and of all their predecessors and successors, and for my own soul, and of Sibyl my wife, and for the souls of all my heirs and ancestors. This land, that is Tomleuchar and Watcarrick, I have given to the aforesaid monks and written in a charter, by these boundaries, namely, as the two Esks meet, so upwards by the Black Esk as far as the first stream that descends from Hartside; and from that stream, upwards through the middle ridge of the mountains, between them and Robert de Brus, as the waters descend towards the monks themselves; and so between the forest of Thimeus and themselves, by the middle ridge of the mountains, as they descend towards themselves; similarly between the land of Bordewich and the monks themselves, by the middle ridge of the mountains, as the waters descend towards them; and so as far as the land of Hawich, and thence across by the middle ridge of the mountains, that is, between myself and the aforesaid monks, as the waters descend towards them; this ridge extends from above Harewude, and so descends to where the two Esks meet. And this whole aforenamed land, possession, and whatever is contained within these aforewritten boundaries, in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in waters and marshes, and in all benefits and easements, I have granted and given, and made firm to the aforesaid monks, in pure and everlasting alms, as a perpetual holding to be held and possessed of me and my heirs, free and quit from all services, and from every secular exaction, as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as any alms is able to be held and possessed. And if by chance anyone should wish to claim the aforesaid alms, I and my heirs shall answer all claimants, and sufficiently protect and warrant it for the aforesaid monks. This is also to be known: that I have retained for myself and my heirs only deer and hind, swine and roebuck, within the aforenamed boundaries, without harm and injury of crops, meadows, hedges, livestock, animals, and all the other possessions of the same monks; provided, however, that I and my heirs shall not have a forester within the described boundaries, nevertheless we shall have within the same boundaries robbers, and eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks, and the guards to guard them, and trespassers; and it should be known that the aforesaid monks shall have and possess the whole aforesaid land, free and quit from me and my heirs, saving my aforesaid wild game, and the eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks; and the monks shall make use of all this tenement according to their will and pleasure, just as it shall seem to them best and most useful to be of use to themselves, their brethren, their servants and livestock. With the exception that they shall not hunt there with packs of hounds and ropes, nor bring others there for hunting, nor set traps there, unless for catching wolves; and they shall not take the eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks within these boundaries. It should be known in addition that the aforenamed monks were accustomed to render to me yearly five merks when I first gave them this land; but after some years, for the honour of God and for the salvation of my soul, I have remitted and given them the five merks in alms to provide for the light before the altar of Saint Mary, for ever; and by the advice of my friends, I have entirely remitted and given to God and the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in pure and everlasting alms, and quitclaimed the four merks which for many years they were accustomed to render to me. Therefore this payment, and the whole aforewritten possession, being agreed and allowed by my heir, Gervase, I have given to God and the aforesaid monks, in free and pure and everlasting alms, quit and released from every service and secular exaction. And that this my alms be established and firm for ever and not able to be brought by any means to nought, I have renewed and made for the same monks this my charter in the time of my lord William, king of the Scots, and my lord William the king, at my petition and request, has confirmed this my donation and alms by his own charter, just as my lord, King Malcolm, previously granted it at my request, and confirmed by his own charter.

These are the witnesses of my first donation and charter which I made for them in the time of King Malcolm: Richard, bishop of St Andrews; Ingram, bishop of Glasgow; Gregory, bishop of Dunkeld; Geoffrey, abbot of Dunfermline; John, abbot of Kelso; Aelred, abbot of Stirling; Nicholas the chancellor; Matthew the archdeacon; Waldef the earl; Duncan the earl; Ferteth the earl; Malcolm the earl; Richard de Morville; Walter son of Alan; David Oliphant; Robert de Quincey; Richard Comyn; Bernard son of Brian; Robert of Berkeley; Walter the clerk; Richard the clerk; Walter of Berkeley. But after that, in the time of my lord William the king, when this donation was renewed, and the aforesaid ferm was remitted in full, these are also the witnesses: Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Simon, archdeacon; Hugh Small, clerk of the king; William, steward of the bishop; Walter, clerk of the bishop; David the earl, brother of the king of the Scots; Alan, steward of the king of the Scots; Robert le Croc, my knight; Alan son of Hugh, the earl's steward; David, Herbert d'Arches; William de Aldri; Gervase de Eincurt, nephew of the lord Robert; Glay, nephew of the same.
